content:
from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 10, 2025 10:09PM
Jurgen Shehaj
In the story, “A Worn Path”, by Eudora Welty, I agree with you that the themes of perseverance and sacrifice are showcased in various examples in the story. I also concur that the goal of her journey was to arrive to the destination of the town in order for her to collect the medication for her ill grandson. It’s funny that you quoted this part of the story, “Well, Granny, you must be a hundred years old, and scared of nothing. (Welty, 1941). This is clearly an example of her perseverance in her journey despite the difficulty of her age along with the difficulties of the journey she would choose to undertake. You are right that her endurance was indeed tested, because she intuitively knows her old age is an obstacle for venturing into the woods in order to retrieve the medication needed for her grandson. I also agree with you that something like this is a test of character because it is during difficult times when deeper traits of the personality such as resilience is tested, and this is clearly evident in the case of Miss Jackson who chose to stead ahead despite whatever came here way. It is true that she also decided to make a sacrifice for her grandson. Her decision to
